name: python-pro
description: Master Python 3.12+ with modern features, async programming,
capabilities: Implement Python 3.12+ features including pattern matching, improved type hints, and performance optimizations
Design and build high-performance async applications with asyncio, aiohttp, and FastAPI
Configure modern Python projects with uv, ruff, mypy, pytest, and pyproject.toml
Optimize Python code for latency, memory usage, and CPU efficiency with profiling tools
Create production-ready APIs with FastAPI, Pydantic validation, and comprehensive error handling
Design scalable data pipelines with Pandas, NumPy, and modern data processing patterns
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only instructional text in SKILL.md with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code, detecting no security patterns. The skill provides Python development guidance and best practices without any file system access, network calls, or command execution. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Python developers struggle with outdated practices and fragmented tooling knowledge. This skill delivers production-ready expertise in Python 3.12+, modern tooling like uv and ruff, async patterns, and performance optimization in one comprehensive resource.
userTitle: Build Modern Python Applications with Expert Guidance
limitations: Cannot execute Python code or install packages in your environment
Requires Python 3.12+ for some advanced features and optimizations
Does not replace formal Python documentation or official library references
Performance recommendations require testing in your specific runtime environment

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building microservices or web APIs
useCases.title: Backend API Development
useCases.description: Build production-ready REST and GraphQL APIs with FastAPI, including authentication, validation, async database access, and automatic OpenAPI documentation.

useCases.targetUser: Technical leads maintaining existing Python applications
useCases.title: Code Modernization
useCases.description: Migrate legacy Python codebases to modern standards including type hints, async patterns, updated dependency management with uv, and ruff-based linting.

useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Profile and optimize Python applications for latency and throughput using cProfile, py-spy, async patterns, and caching strategies.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Set Up Modern Python Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a new Python project with modern tooling. I need uv for package management, ruff for linting and formatting, mypy for type checking, and pytest for testing. Generate the pyproject.toml configuration and explain each tool's purpose.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new Python project with current best practices

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Convert to Async
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this synchronous Python code and refactor it to use async/await patterns with asyncio. Identify which operations are I/O-bound and would benefit from async. Include proper error handling and explain the performance implications.
promptTemplates.scenario: Optimizing I/O-bound code for better concurrency

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Type-Safe API Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a FastAPI application with full type safety using Pydantic v2 models, dependency injection, custom exception handlers, and async database access with SQLAlchemy 2.0. Include comprehensive test examples with pytest fixtures.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building production APIs with robust type checking

promptTemplates.title: Expert: Performance Profiling
promptTemplates.prompt: Profile this Python application to identify bottlenecks. Recommend specific optimizations for memory usage, CPU-bound operations, and database queries. Include before/after benchmarks and explain the trade-offs of each optimization strategy.

bestPractices: Use type hints throughout your codebase with mypy strict mode for early error detection
Prefer composition over inheritance and follow SOLID principles for maintainable code
Write tests with pytest achieving greater than 90 percent coverage for critical paths
antiPatterns: Avoid bare except clauses that catch all exceptions including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt
Do not use mutable default arguments in function definitions
Never store secrets in source code or use environment variables without validation

faq.question: What Python version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill specializes in Python 3.12+ with full coverage of latest features. It can provide guidance for older versions but optimizes for current best practices.

faq.question: Can this skill help migrate from pip to uv?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides step-by-step migration guidance from pip or poetry to uv, including commands for dependency installation, lock file generation, and CI integration.

faq.question: Does this skill write executable code?
faq.answer: This skill provides code examples and guidance only. It cannot execute code, install packages, or modify your files directly. All code should be tested in your environment.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Primary focus is pytest with its ecosystem including pytest-asyncio, pytest-cov, and pytest-benchmark. It also covers unittest, Hypothesis for property-based testing, and integration testing patterns.

faq.question: Can this skill help with async debugging?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers async debugging techniques including proper exception handling, task cancellation, deadlock prevention, and tools like asyncio-debug and structured logging for async applications.

faq.question: Does this skill cover data science libraries?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes NumPy, Pandas, and visualization libraries with focus on performance optimization, memory efficiency, and integration with web frameworks for data APIs.
